title: Ali Accepts Allegiance from the People and the Account of Ziyad b. Abi Sufyan and 'Abd al-Rahman b. Abi Bakrah
description: Account of Ziyad b. Abi Sufyan1061 and `Abd al-Rahman b. Abi Bakrah These words were said in the allegiance: "The fulfillment of Allah's promise and covenant is obligatory on you. Our peace will be your peace, and our war will be your war. You will restrain your tongue and hand from attacking us." Now Ziyad b. Abi Sufyan was one of those who withdrew and did not take part in the battle. He stayed back in the house of Nafi` b. al-Harith. `Abd al-Rahman b.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 16
